content.content:
=== PLAN ===
Build a browser-based Yew/Rust/WASM Pascal Compiler IDE for COR24 p-code VM. Read-only Pascal source and pre-compiled p-code assembly side by side, with link/assemble/run entirely in WASM. Phase 1: scaffold Yew project with Trunk, wire cor24-emulator + pa24r + pl24r dependencies, build.rs to pre-assemble pvm.s. Phase 2: demo registry — embed .pas + .spc pairs via include_str!(), demo selector dropdown. Phase 3: UI layout — four-panel grid (Pascal source, p-code assembly, output, hardware), Catppuccin Mocha theme. Phase 4: Link & Run pipeline — pl24r linking, pa24r assembly, loader (load .p24 at 0x010000, relocate data refs), batch execution. Phase 5: I/O and hardware — UART output display, UART input field, LED D2 indicator, S2 switch toggle. Phase 6: status bar + polish — Ready/Running/Exited states, instruction count, binary size, Stop button.

=== STEPS ===
  [x] 001-scaffold-yew-trunk [legacy]: Scaffold the Yew/Trunk/WASM project. Study web-dv24r's Cargo.toml, build.rs, ...
  [x] 002-002-demo-registry [legacy]: Build the demo registry: embed .pas + .spc pairs via include_str\!(). Study w...
  [x] 003-003-ui-layout [production]: Phase 3: Four-panel grid layout with Catppuccin Mocha theme. Study web-dv24r'...
  [x] 004-004-link-and-run [production]: Phase 4: Link & Run pipeline. Study web-dv24r's execution model for pattern r...
  [x] 005-005-io-and-hardware [production]: Phase 5: I/O and hardware. Implement: (1) UART output display — show TX byt...
  [ ] 006-006-live-compilation [production]: Phase 6: In-browser Pascal compilation. p24p now has UART-input mode (src/mai... <-- YOU ARE HERE

=== CURRENT STEP: 006-006-live-compilation [production] ===
Status: pending
Description: Phase 6: In-browser Pascal compilation. p24p now has UART-input mode (src/mai...

=== PROMPT ===
Phase 6: In-browser Pascal compilation. p24p now has UART-input mode (src/main.c reads Pascal via getchar until EOT, compiles to .spc on UART). Integrate this into web-p24c:

1. Build-time: Add to build.rs — compile p24p C source with tc24r to COR24 assembly, then assemble to binary (same pattern as pvm.s). Embed the p24p binary via include_bytes!().

2. Runtime: Add a 'Compile & Run' mode alongside existing 'Link & Run':
   - User edits Pascal source in a text area (left panel becomes editable)
   - Click 'Compile & Run' spins up a second EmulatorCore running the p24p binary
   - Feed Pascal source + EOT via UART to the p24p emulator
   - Run in batches until halt, collecting .spc from UART output
   - Feed collected .spc into existing pipeline: pl24r link with runtime → pa24r assemble → loader → pvm.s execute

3. UI changes:
   - Pascal source panel becomes editable text area when in compile mode
   - P-code panel shows the .spc output from compilation (read-only)
   - Status shows Compiling → Linking → Running progression
   - Keep demo selector for pre-compiled demos as fast defaults

Reference: p24p UART mode documented in wiki [[P24P]]. Build pattern: study web-p24c/build.rs for pvm.s pre-assembly. Emulator batch execution pattern: study web-p24c/src/lib.rs Msg::Tick handler.

Dependencies: tc24r must be available at build time. p24p source at ~/github/softwarewrighter/p24p/src/{main.c,lexer.c,lexer.h,parser.c,parser.h}. tc24r include dir at ~/github/sw-vibe-coding/tc24r/include.

=== WHEN DONE ===
Run: agentrail begin    (if not yet started)
Run: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --next-slug <slug> --next-prompt "next instructions"
  Or: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --done   (if this is the last step)

content.text: Starting Phase 6: In-browser Pascal compilation. Let me study the current codebase and dependencies first.

content.content:
1→//! Demo registry — Pascal source + pre-compiled p-code assembly pairs.
     2→//!
     3→//! Each demo embeds a .pas file (Pascal source, displayed read-only) and
     4→//! a .spc file (pre-compiled p-code assembly, fed to linker at runtime).
     5→//! The runtime library .spc is also embedded for linking.
     6→
     7→/// A demo entry with Pascal source and p-code assembly.
     8→pub struct Demo {
     9→    pub name: &'static str,
    10→    pub description: &'static str,
    11→    pub pas_source: &'static str,
    12→    pub spc_source: &'static str,
    13→}
    14→
    15→/// Runtime library .spc source (linked with every demo).
    16→pub const RUNTIME_SPC: &str = include_str!("../../pr24p/src/runtime.spc");
    17→
    18→/// Available demo programs.
    19→pub const DEMOS: &[Demo] = &[
    20→    Demo {
    21→        name: "Hello World",
    22→        description: "Print Hello, World! and a newline",
    23→        pas_source: include_str!("../demos/hello.pas"),
    24→        spc_source: include_str!("../demos/hello.spc"),
    25→    },
    26→    Demo {
    27→        name: "Countdown",
    28→        description: "Count down from 5 to 1",
    29→        pas_source: include_str!("../demos/countdown.pas"),
    30→        spc_source: include_str!("../demos/countdown.spc"),
    31→    },
    32→];
    33→

1→[package]
     2→name = "web-p24c"
     3→version = "0.1.0"
     4→edition = "2024"
     5→authors = ["Michael A Wright"]
     6→license = "MIT"
     7→description = "Browser-based Pascal Demos for COR24"
     8→
     9→[lib]
    10→crate-type = ["cdylib", "rlib"]
    11→
    12→[dependencies]
    13→cor24-emulator = { path = "../../sw-embed/cor24-rs", default-features = false }
    14→pa24r = { path = "../pa24r" }
    15→pl24r = { path = "../pl24r" }
    16→yew = { version = "0.21", features = ["csr"] }
    17→wasm-bindgen = "0.2"
    18→web-sys = { version = "0.3", features = ["console", "HtmlTextAreaElement", "HtmlInputElement", "HtmlSelectElement", "HtmlElement", "KeyboardEvent"] }
    19→gloo = "0.11"
    20→console_error_panic_hook = "0.1"
    21→
    22→[build-dependencies]
    23→cor24-emulator = { path = "../../sw-embed/cor24-rs", default-features = false }
    24→
    25→[profile.release]
    26→opt-level = "z"
    27→lto = true
    28→

file.content:
[package]
name = "web-p24c"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2024"
authors = ["Michael A Wright"]
license = "MIT"
description = "Browser-based Pascal Demos for COR24"

[lib]
crate-type = ["cdylib", "rlib"]

[dependencies]
cor24-emulator = { path = "../../sw-embed/cor24-rs", default-features = false }
pa24r = { path = "../pa24r" }
pl24r = { path = "../pl24r" }
yew = { version = "0.21", features = ["csr"] }
wasm-bindgen = "0.2"
web-sys = { version = "0.3", features = ["console", "HtmlTextAreaElement", "HtmlInputElement", "HtmlSelectElement", "HtmlElement", "KeyboardEvent"] }
gloo = "0.11"
console_error_panic_hook = "0.1"

[build-dependencies]
cor24-emulator = { path = "../../sw-embed/cor24-rs", default-features = false }

[profile.release]
opt-level = "z"
lto = true

lexer.h
main.c
parser.c
parser.h

content.text: Now let me check tc24r and p24p details, plus the app.css for UI reference.

input.prompt:
Quick exploration: I need to understand how tc24r compiles C to COR24 assembly.

1. Check ~/github/sw-vibe-coding/tc24r - look at Cargo.toml for the crate structure and any lib.rs or main.rs to understand the public API
2. Specifically find if tc24r has a Rust API (lib.rs with public functions) that can compile C source to COR24 assembly, or if it's only a CLI tool
3. Check the include directory at ~/github/sw-vibe-coding/tc24r/include - what files are there?
4. Look at how any other build.rs in web-* projects uses tc24r (search for "tc24r" in build.rs files under ~/github)

Return: the tc24r public API for compiling C, the include files available, and any build.rs patterns that use tc24r.

content.text:
Excellent! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile my findings:

## tc24r Compilation Pipeline: Public API Summary

Based on my exploration of the tc24r project, here's how C is compiled to COR24 assembly:

### **Rust Public API for Compilation**

tc24r **is primarily a CLI tool, NOT a library**. There is no public `lib.rs` — only a binary at `/Users/mike/github/sw-vibe-coding/tc24r/components/cli/crates/tc24r/src/main.rs`.

However, the underlying **compilation pipeline components ARE exposed as Rust libraries**:

**Compilation Flow:**
1. **tc24r-preprocess** (`~preprocess.rs`)
   ```rust
   pub fn preprocess(source: &str, source_dir: Option<&Path>, system_paths: &[&Path]) -> String
   ```
   - Handles `#define`, `#include` (both `"file"` and `<file>`), and `#pragma once`
   - Returns preprocessed source code

2. **tc24r-lexer** (`~scanner.rs`)
   ```rust
   pub struct Lexer<'a> { ... }
   impl<'a> Lexer<'a> {
       pub fn new(source: &'a str) -> Self { ... }
       pub fn tokenize(&mut self) -> Result<Vec<Token>, CompileError> { ... }
   }
   ```
   - Converts text → tokens

3. **tc24r-parser** (`~/lib.rs`)
   ```rust
   pub fn parse(tokens: Vec<Token>) -> Result<Program, CompileError>
   ```
   - Converts tokens → AST (tc24r_ast::Program)

4. **tc24r-codegen** (`~codegen.rs`)
   ```rust
   pub struct Codegen { pub state: CodegenState }
   impl Codegen {
       pub fn new() -> Self { ... }
       pub fn generate(&mut self, program: &Program) -> String { ... }
   }
   ```
   - Converts AST → COR24 assembly string

**Minimal compilation example (library style):**
```rust
let source = std::fs::read_to_string("program.c")?;
let preprocessed = tc24r_preprocess::preprocess(&source, None, &[]);
let tokens = tc24r_lexer::Lexer::new(&preprocessed).tokenize()?;
let program = tc24r_parser::parse(tokens)?;
let assembly = tc24r_codegen::Codegen::new().generate(&program);
std::fs::write("output.s", assembly)?;
```

---

### **Include Files Available**

Located at `/Users/mike/github/sw-vibe-coding/tc24r/include/`:

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| **cor24.h** | COR24-TB hardware MMIO registers (LED_REG, UART_DATA, UART_STATUS, INT_ENABLE) |
| **stdio.h** | printf-like stubs |
| **stdlib.h** | malloc, free, rand stubs |
| **string.h** | strlen, strcpy, strcmp, memcpy, memset stubs |
| **stdbool.h** | bool, true, false definitions |
| **test.h** | Test framework macros |

These are included with `-I /Users/mike/github/sw-vibe-coding/tc24r/include` when compiling.

---

### **Build.rs Patterns in web-* Projects**

**Current usage in web-p24c/build.rs** (at `/Users/mike/github/softwarewrighter/web-p24c/build.rs`):
- Does **NOT** use tc24r directly
- Instead uses `cor24_emulator::Assembler` to pre-assemble `asm/pvm.s` at build time
- Extracts label addresses and writes them as Rust source code
- This suggests web-p24c runs COR24 code through an emulator, not raw tc24r compilation

**No build.rs patterns found** that invoke tc24r CLI or link tc24r libraries in the softwarewrighter web-* projects.

---

### **CLI Usage (Practical)**

```bash
tc24r <input.c> [-o output.s] [-I dir]
```
- Default output: stdout
- Supports multiple `-I` paths for include directories
- Available at: `components/cli/target/release/tc24r` (after building)

file.content:
#pragma once

/* p24p Phase 1 Lexer — tokenizes Pascal source from a char buffer */

#define MAX_LEXEME 64
#define MAX_STRING 256

/* Token types — Phase 0 keywords */
#define TOK_PROGRAM    0
#define TOK_CONST      1
#define TOK_VAR        2
#define TOK_BEGIN      3
#define TOK_END        4
#define TOK_IF         5
#define TOK_THEN       6
#define TOK_ELSE       7
#define TOK_WHILE      8
#define TOK_DO         9
#define TOK_WRITELN    10
#define TOK_INTEGER_KW 11
#define TOK_BOOLEAN_KW 12
#define TOK_TRUE       13
#define TOK_FALSE      14
#define TOK_DIV        15
#define TOK_MOD        16
#define TOK_AND        17
#define TOK_OR         18
#define TOK_NOT        19
/* Phase 1 keywords */
#define TOK_PROCEDURE  40
#define TOK_FUNCTION   41
#define TOK_FORWARD    42
#define TOK_TYPE       43
#define TOK_ARRAY      44
#define TOK_OF         45
#define TOK_RECORD     46
#define TOK_CHAR_KW    47
#define TOK_FOR        48
#define TOK_TO         49
#define TOK_DOWNTO     50
#define TOK_REPEAT     51
#define TOK_UNTIL      52
#define TOK_CASE       53
#define TOK_WRITE      54
#define TOK_READ       55
#define TOK_READLN     56
/* Symbols */
#define TOK_ASSIGN     20
#define TOK_SEMI       21
#define TOK_DOT        22
#define TOK_COMMA      23
#define TOK_LPAREN     24
#define TOK_RPAREN     25
#define TOK_PLUS       26
#define TOK_MINUS      27
#define TOK_STAR       28
#define TOK_EQ         29
#define TOK_NEQ        30
#define TOK_LT         31
#define TOK_LE         32
#define TOK_GT         33
#define TOK_GE         34
#define TOK_COLON      35
#define TOK_LBRACKET   57
#define TOK_RBRACKET   58
#define TOK_DOTDOT     59
/* Literals */
#define TOK_IDENT      36
#define TOK_INT_LIT    37
#define TOK_CHAR_LIT   60
#define TOK_STR_LIT    61
/* Special */
#define TOK_EOF        38
#define TOK_ERROR      39

/* Current token state — set by next_token() */
int tok_type;
int tok_line;
int tok_int_val;
char tok_lexeme[MAX_LEXEME];
char tok_str_val[MAX_STRING];
int tok_str_len;

/* Lexer source state */
char *lex_src;
int lex_pos;
int lex_len;
int lex_line;

/* Initialize lexer with source buffer and its length */
void lexer_init(char *src, int len);

/* Advance to next token. Returns token type. */
int next_token(void);

/* Return printable name for a token type */
char *token_name(int type);

content.content:
1→#pragma once
     2→
     3→/* p24p Phase 0 Parser — recursive descent, emits .spc directly */
     4→
     5→/* Type kinds */
     6→#define TYPE_INTEGER 0
     7→#define TYPE_BOOLEAN 1
     8→
     9→/* Symbol kinds */
    10→#define SYM_CONST 0
    11→#define SYM_VAR   1
    12→
    13→/* Symbol table limits */
    14→#define MAX_SYMBOLS 64
    15→#define MAX_NAME 32
    16→#define SYM_NAME_SIZE 2048
    17→
    18→/* Symbol table — parallel arrays (avoids tc24r struct array bugs) */
    19→char sym_name[SYM_NAME_SIZE];
    20→int sym_kind[MAX_SYMBOLS];
    21→int sym_type_id[MAX_SYMBOLS];
    22→int sym_value[MAX_SYMBOLS];
    23→int sym_count;
    24→
    25→/* Label counter */
    26→int label_count;
    27→
    28→/* Error flag */
    29→int parse_error;
    30→
    31→/* Initialize parser with source buffer */
    32→void parser_init(char *src, int len);
    33→
    34→/* Parse a complete Pascal program, emitting .spc to stdout */
    35→void parse_program(void);
    36→

git add build.rs src/app.css src/config.rs src/lib.rs .agentrail/saga.toml .agentrail/steps/006-006-live-compilation/ && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: in-browser Pascal compilation via p24p emulator

Compile p24p (Pascal compiler) C sources with tc24r at build time,
assemble to COR24 binary, and embed for runtime use. Add Compile mode
with editable Pascal source textarea, Demo/Compile mode tabs, and
Compile & Run button that spins up a second EmulatorCore running p24p
to compile user Pascal source via UART, then feeds the .spc output
through the existing pl24r/pa24r/pvm.s pipeline.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
